content.problemStatement:
You are given a strictly increasing sequence of positive integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč. It is also guaranteed that for each valid iii, Ai+A1‚Č•Ai+1A_i+A_1 \ge A_{i+1}Ai‚Äč+A1‚Äč‚Č•Ai+1‚Äč.

Alice and Bob want to play QQQ independent games using this sequence. Before they play these games, Alice should choose a positive integer GGG between 111 and MMM (inclusive). The rules of each game are as follows:


There is a set SSS of positive integers representing allowed moves in this game. For each valid iii, in the iii-th game, this set is S={ALi,ALi+1,‚Ä¶,ARi}S = \{A_{L_i},A_{L_i+1},\ldots,A_{R_i}\}S={ALi‚Äč‚Äč,ALi‚Äč+1‚Äč,‚Ä¶,ARi‚Äč‚Äč} (in other words, given by a contiguous subsequence of AAA).
The game is played with a single pile. Let's denote the number of objects in this pile by PPP. At the beginning of the game, P=GP = GP=G. In other words, Alice and Bob play all the games with the same initial number of objects that Alice chooses.
The players alternate turns; Alice plays first.
In each turn, the current player must choose a positive integer bbb from the set SSS such that b‚Č§Pb \le Pb‚Č§P and remove bbb objects from the pile, i.e. change PPP to P‚ąíbP-bP‚ąíb.
When the current player cannot choose any such integer bbb, this player loses this game.
Both players play optimally. You need to find the maximum number of games Alice can win if she chooses GGG optimally.
constraints: Ai+A1‚Č•Ai+1>AiA_i+A_1 \ge A_{i+1} \gt A_iAi‚Äč+A1‚Äč‚Č•Ai+1‚Äč>Ai‚Äč for each valid iii
1‚Č§Li‚Č§Ri‚Č§N1 \le L_i \le R_i \le N1‚Č§Li‚Äč‚Č§Ri‚Äč‚Č§N for each valid iii
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚čÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚čÖ105
the sum of QQQ over all test cases does not exceed 2‚čÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚čÖ105
the sum of MMM over all test cases does not exceed 2‚čÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚čÖ105
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 400400400
the sum of QQQ over all test cases does not exceed 400400400
the sum of MMM over all test cases does not exceed 400400400
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 5,0005,0005,000
the sum of QQQ over all test cases does not exceed 5,0005,0005,000
the sum of MMM over all test cases does not exceed 5,0005,0005,000
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN, QQQ and MMM.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.
QQQ lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains two space-separated integers LiL_iLi‚Äč and RiR_iRi‚Äč.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äē the maximum number of games Alice can win.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: The set of allowed moves in the first game is S={2,3}S = \{2,3\}S={2,3} and in the second game, it is S={3,4}S = \{3,4\}S={3,4}. If Alice chooses G=3G=3G=3, she can win both games.
